channel.title: Kubernetes on Terra Nullius
channel.description: Recent content in Kubernetes on Terra Nullius
channel.copyright: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License

item.title: Kubernetes and Secrets Management in Cloud
item.description: Introduction Secrets are essential for operation of many production systems. Unintended secrets exposure is one of the top risks that should properly addressed. Most companies must do their best to protect their secrets. The problem becomes even harder, once company moves to a microservice architecture and multiple services need different secrets in order to work. How to distribute, manage, monitor and rotate these secrets, avoiding unintended leaks? Kubernetes Secrets Kubernetes provides a resource called Secret, which you can use to store sensitive data, like passwords, SSH keys, API keys, tokens and other.

item.title: Continuous Delivery and Continuous Deployment for Kubernetes microservices
item.description: Continuous Delivery and Continuous Deployment for Kubernetes microservices THIS IS A DRAFT VERSION OF POST TO COME, PLEASE DO NOT SHARE Starting Point Over last years we've been adopting several concepts for our project, straggling to make them work together. The first one is the Microservice Architecture. We did not start it clean and by the book, rather applied it to the already existing project: splitting big services into smaller and breaking excessive coupling.
